connections.gloss: chief ruler, [idiom] own, priest, prince, principal officer.
chief (high) priest, chief of the priests
connections.def: from 746 and 2409; the high-priest (literally, of the Jews, typically, Christ); by extension a chief priest:--chief (high) priest, chief of the priests. see GREEK for 746 see GREEK for

connections.gloss: deck, be (do the office of a, execute the, minister in the) priest('s office).

connections.def: from 2413; a priest (literally or figuratively):--(high) priest. see GREEK for
{one officiating, a priest; also (by courtesy) an acting priest (although a layman)}
